This bill was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. The bill prevents tax increases scheduled for 2025 by making individual tax cuts permanent.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. This change effectively makes meeting domestic-content rules a condition to claim the 45Y Production Tax Credit. Therefore, in 2025, the domestic content requirements became mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y).

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including those for clean electricity investment. The Act specifies domestic content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit (Section 48E), making these requirements mandatory for obtaining the Investment Tax Credits (ITC). For example, it sets a domestic content percentage of 45% for projects starting construction after June 16, 2025, and before January 1, 2026, and 50% for projects starting construction in 2026.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that effectively require the use of domestic materials or subcomponents for certain tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit.
